# outprep

> Scout any chess player, study their openings and weaknesses, then practice against a bot that plays like them.

outprep is a free chess preparation platform at [outprep.xyz](https://outprep.xyz). Search for any FIDE-rated player (80,000+), Lichess user, or Chess.com user, analyze their opening repertoire and weaknesses, then play against an AI bot calibrated to mimic their style using Stockfish with Boltzmann-weighted move selection.

## Player Sources

outprep works with multiple types of players:

### FIDE (Over-the-Board) Players
- 80,000+ FIDE-rated players with OTB tournament games from TWIC (The Week in Chess)
- Search by **name** (e.g. "Carlsen") or **FIDE ID** (e.g. "1503014") using the search bar on the home page
- FIDE player profiles live at `/player/{slug}` (e.g. `/player/carlsen-magnus-1503014`)
- You can practice against any FIDE player's OTB style

### Lichess Players
- Any public Lichess account can be scouted
- Enter the player's **exact Lichess username** in the search bar and submit
- Lichess player profiles live at `/player/lichess:{username}` (e.g. `/player/lichess:DrNykterstein`)
- You can practice against the player at `/play/{username}`

### Chess.com Players
- Any public Chess.com account can be scouted
- Enter the player's **exact Chess.com username** in the search bar and submit
- Chess.com player profiles live at `/player/chesscom:{username}` (e.g. `/player/chesscom:hikaru`)

### PGN Upload
- You can also upload PGN files directly to scout any player not on Lichess, Chess.com, or FIDE
- PGN uploads are available at `/player/pgn:{playerName}`

## Key Features

- **Player Scouting**: Opening repertoire analysis, error profiles by game phase, weakness identification, and preparation tips — for FIDE, Lichess, and Chess.com players.
- **Practice Mode**: Play against a Stockfish-powered bot tuned to replicate a specific player's opening choices, tactical tendencies, and mistake patterns.
- **Post-Game Analysis**: Move-by-move evaluation with position classification, narrative feedback, and opponent context.
- **PGN Upload**: Import your own games or your opponent's games for analysis and practice.

## Pages

- [Home](https://outprep.xyz/): Search for FIDE players (by name or FIDE ID), Lichess users, or Chess.com users. Upload PGN files, browse top players.
- [Player (FIDE)](https://outprep.xyz/player/{slug}): FIDE player profile with stats, openings, recent OTB games, scouting features, and practice link
- [Player (Lichess)](https://outprep.xyz/player/lichess:{username}): Lichess player scouting report — openings, weaknesses, prep tips
- [Player (Chess.com)](https://outprep.xyz/player/chesscom:{username}): Chess.com player scouting report
- [Play](https://outprep.xyz/play/{username}): Play against an AI bot mimicking a player
- [Game](https://outprep.xyz/game/{slug}): Individual FIDE game with replay
- [Analysis](https://outprep.xyz/analysis/{gameId}): Post-game analysis with move evaluations and narrative

## API

- `GET /api/players/search?q={query}`: Search FIDE players by name or FIDE ID
- `GET /api/profile/{username}`: Player profile (rating, title, stats)
- `GET /api/bot-data/{username}`: Bot data including error profile and opening tries
- `GET /api/opening-book/{username}`: Opening repertoire analysis
- `GET /api/fide-games/{slug}`: Individual FIDE game data
- `GET /api/fide-practice/{slug}`: Start practice session against a FIDE player
- `POST /api/analysis`: Analyze a completed game

## Tech Stack

- Next.js (App Router), React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- Stockfish 17 WASM (client-side chess engine)
- PostgreSQL, Vercel Blob storage
- Lichess API, Chess.com API, and FIDE/TWIC data pipeline

## How the Bot Works

The bot engine builds a playing profile from a player's real games:
1. **Opening book**: Frequency-weighted move trees from the player's game history
2. **Error profile**: Per-phase (opening/middlegame/endgame) mistake rates calibrated to their Elo
3. **Move selection**: Boltzmann distribution over Stockfish evaluations, with temperature scaled to match the player's error tendencies
4. **Complexity awareness**: Depth and randomness adjust based on position complexity
